title: Hot Water Beach Review
meta_desc: Discover the unique Hot Water Beach in Coromandel, NZ. Free admission, suitable for all ages, and a one-of-a-kind experience. Read our honest family review.
intro: Hot Water Beach in Coromandel is a must-visit destination for families. This unique beach offers a free and fun experience for all ages. With geothermal water seeping through the sand at low tide, you can dig your own hot pool right on the beach.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Hot Water Beach is a truly unique experience. At low tide, geothermal water seeps through the sand, allowing you to dig your own hot pool. The water can reach temperatures of up to 64°C, so be sure to mix in cold seawater to create a comfortable temperature. You can hire a spade for $5 to dig your own pool.

The experience is suitable for all ages, from 0+, making it an ideal activity for families with young children. The beach is stroller-friendly, and there are toilet facilities nearby.
list: Mix in cold seawater for a comfortable temperature

sections.heading: Value for Money
sections.body:
With free admission for both adults and children, Hot Water Beach offers excellent value for money. The only cost is the $5 spade hire, which is a small price to pay for this unique experience.

Considering the experience can last anywhere from 1.5 to 2.5 hours, it's a great way to spend a morning or afternoon with your family.

sections.heading: Insider Tips
sections.body:
To make the most of your visit, be sure to check the low tide times in advance. The experience only works 2 hours either side of low tide, so plan your visit accordingly.

Avoid visiting during peak summer months if you can, as the beach can get crowded. Instead, opt for the shoulder season when the weather is still warm but the crowds are smaller.
list: Check low tide times in advance

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit
sections.body:
The best time to visit Hot Water Beach is during the shoulder season, from September to November or from March to May. The weather is still warm, but the crowds are smaller, making for a more enjoyable experience.

Be sure to check the low tide times and plan your visit accordingly. It's also a good idea to arrive early to secure parking and avoid the crowds.

sections.heading: Booking and Saving Money
sections.body:
There's no need to book in advance for Hot Water Beach, as admission is free. Simply arrive at the beach, hire a spade for $5, and start digging your own hot pool.

To save money, consider visiting during the shoulder season when the crowds are smaller. You can also pack a picnic lunch and enjoy it on the beach, rather than buying food from the nearby cafes.
